questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A liquid $(X)$ of molecular mass 77 on steam distillation at 760 mmHg atmospheric pressure gave a distillate containing $25 \%$ by mass of $(X)$. What is the vapour pressure of $(X)$ in mmHg at the temperature of distillation to the nearest integer?
questions.solution:
For immiscible liquids $(X)$ and water, (at the boiling temperature) if P is the vapour pressure of liquid $(X)$ then
$\frac{P_x}{P_w}=\frac{n_x}{n_w}$
$\frac{P}{760-P}=\frac{\left(W_x\right)\left(M_w\right)}{M_x W_w}=\frac{25 \times 18}{77 \times 75}$

On solving $P=55 \mathrm{mmHg}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A sample of air (containing $20 \% \mathrm{O}_2$ and $80 \% \mathrm{~N}_2$ ) is dissolved in water when total pressure of air is 1 atm . The solubility of $\mathrm{N}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_2$ ( in terms of mole fraction) is x and y . This sample is taken at a height of H above sea level where pressure of air is 0.4 atm and temperature is 300 K and solubility of $\mathrm{N}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_2$ (in terms of mole fraction) is $\mathrm{x}^{\prime}$ and $\mathrm{y}^{\prime}$. Assume that value of Henry's constant remains same at sea level and H .
The value of $2\left(\frac{x}{x^{\prime}}+\frac{y}{y^{\prime}}\right)$ is
questions.solution:
Hint : $p_A \propto x_A$
Solution :
At sea level
$\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{N}_2}=0.8 \quad \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{O}_2}=0.2$
at height $\mathrm{H}, \mathrm{P}=0.4 \mathrm{~atm}$
Mole fraction of $\mathrm{N}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_2$ remain in ratio of $4: 1$ at any height.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{N}_2}=0.4 \times 0.8=0.32 \mathrm{~atm} \\
& \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{O}_2}=0.4 \times 0.2=0.08 \mathrm{~atm} \\
& \mathrm{x}=\frac{0.8}{\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{h}}} \\
& \mathrm{x}^{\prime}=\frac{0.32}{\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{h}}} \\
& \begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{x}^{\prime}}=2.5 \\
\text { and } \frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{y}^{\prime}}=2.5 \\
\therefore \quad 2\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{x}^{\prime}}+\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{y}^{\prime}}\right) \\
\quad=10
\end{aligned}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 32 g of hydrated magnesium sulphate $\mathrm{MgSO}_4 \cdot \mathrm{xH}_2 \mathrm{O}$, when dissolved in 84 g of water, the solution freezes at $-4.836{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. If $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}=1.86 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $\mathrm{MgSO}_4$ is a strong electrolyte, what is the value of x ?
questions.solution:
molar mass of salt $=120+18 x$
mass of water present in the salt $=\frac{18 \mathrm{x}}{120+18 \mathrm{x}} \times 32 \mathrm{~g}$

Molality of the solution $=\frac{32}{120+18 x} \times\left[\frac{1000}{84+\frac{18 \times 32 x}{120+18 x}}\right]$
$=\frac{4000}{1260+261 x}$
$\begin{gathered}
\Delta T_f=4.836=2 \times 1.86 \times \frac{4000}{1260+261 x} \\
x=6.9 \approx 7
\end{gathered}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: In how many cases mixing of liquid $(A)$ and liquid $(B)$ is more effective if liquid $(A)$ is $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ and liquid (B) can be $\mathrm{HCl}, \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}, \mathrm{HNO}_3$ and $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OCH}_3$
questions.solution: In $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ and $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}$ mixing shows +ve deviation while in other cases it shows -ve deviation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The difference in height of the mercury column in two arms of $U$ tube manometer in arrangement-I is $h_1=660$ mm . In another arrangement-II at same temperature, 222 gm of $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ is dissolved in 324 gm of water and difference in height of mercury column in two arms is found to be $h_2=680 \mathrm{~mm}$. If the value of degree of dissociation for $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ in arrangement-II is $\alpha$ then the value of $6.4 \alpha$ is: [Atmospheric pressure $=1 \mathrm{~atm}$ ]
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{CaCl}_2}=111 \mathrm{~g}$
$\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{CaCl}_2}=\frac{222}{111}=2 \text { mole } \quad \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}=\frac{324}{18}=18 \mathrm{~mole}$

Relative lowering in vapour pressure
$\text { R.L.V.P. }=\frac{P_0-P_S}{P_0}=\frac{n_1 i}{n_1 i+n_2}=\frac{100-80}{100}=\frac{i \times 2}{i \times 2+18}$
or $\quad 0.2=\frac{2 i}{2 i+18}$ or $0.4 i+3.6=2 i$
$\mathrm{i}=2.25$

So $\mathrm{i}=2.25$
For $\mathrm{CaCl}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{i}=1+(\mathrm{n}-1) \alpha$
$\begin{aligned}
& 2.25=1+(3-1) \alpha \\
& \alpha=\frac{1.25}{2}=0.625
\end{aligned}$
$6.4 \times 0.625=4$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If the sum of mole fraction of NaOH in its aqueous solution and the mole fraction of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ in an another aqueous solution of KOH is equal to one, find the molality of KOH solution if the molality of NaOH solution is ' m '.
Report your answer as ' Y ' where $\mathrm{Y}=$ (Molality of KOH solution) $\div$ (Molality of NaOH solution)
questions.solution:
Let mole fraction of NaOH in its aqueous solution be X .
$\therefore$ Mole fraction of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ in an aqueous solution of $\mathrm{KOH}=1-\mathrm{X}$ so, mole fraction of KOH in its aqueous solution $=1-(1-X)=X$ Thus, both aqueous NaOH solution and aqueous KOH solution have same mole fraction of solute.
$\text { Now, molality }=\frac{\mathrm{X}_{\text {solute } \times 1000}}{\left(1-\mathrm{X}_{\text {solute }}\right) \times \text { Mol.Mass }_{\text {solvent }}}$

Since both solutions have same solvent $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)$ and same mole fraction of solute, so they will have same molality
$\therefore$ Molality of aq. KOH solution $=\mathrm{m}$
$\therefore Y=m / m=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Vapour pressure of an equimolar mixture of benzene and toluene at a given temperature was found to be 80 mm Hg . If vapour above the liquid phase is condensed in a beaker, vapour pressure of this condensate at the same temperature was found to be 100 mm Hg . If the pure state vapour pressure of benzene and toluene is respectively $x$ and $y$. Then determine value of $\frac{x+2 y}{50}$ :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & X_B=0.5 \quad X_T=0.5 \\ & p_{\text {total }}=P_B^{\circ} \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{B}}+\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}^{\circ} \cdot \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{T}} \\ & 80=0.5 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\circ}+0.5 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}^{\circ}\end{aligned}$
$160=\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\circ}+\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}^{\circ}$   ...(1)
$\begin{aligned} & y_B=\frac{P_B^{\circ} \cdot X_B}{p_{\text {total }}}=\frac{P_{B \cdot}^{\circ} \times 0.5}{80}=\frac{P_B^{\circ}}{160} \\ & y_T=\frac{P_T^{\circ} \cdot x_T}{p_{\text {total }}}=\frac{P_T^{\circ} \cdot \times 0.5}{80}=\frac{P_T^{\circ}}{160} \\ & p_{\text {total }}^{\prime}=100=P_B^{\circ} \cdot y_B+P_T^{\circ} \cdot y_T\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \quad=100=P_B^{\circ} \cdot \frac{P_B^{\circ}}{160}+P_T^{\circ} \cdot \frac{P_T^{\circ}}{160} \\ & P_B^{\circ^2}+P_T^{\circ^2}=100 \times 160\end{aligned}$

From (1) Eq.
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(P_B^{\circ}+P_T^{\circ}\right)=160 \\
& \left(P_B^{\circ}+P_T^{\circ}\right)^2=160^2=P_B^{0^2}+P_T^{0^2}+2 P_B^{\circ} \times P_T^{\circ} \\
& =P_B^{\circ^2}+P_T^{\circ^2}+2 P_B^{\circ} P_T^{\circ} \\
& =100 \times 160+2 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\circ} \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}^{\circ} \\
& 2 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\circ} \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}^{\circ}=160^2-160 \times 100=160 \times 60 \\
& P_B^{\circ} P_T^{\circ}=160 \times 30 ; \quad P_B^{\circ}+P_T^{\circ}=160 \\
& P_B^{\circ}=120=x \quad ; \quad P_T^{\circ}=40=y \\
& \therefore \frac{x+2 y}{50}=4
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An aqueous solution containing 46.5 gm of ethylene glycol in 160 gm of water is cooled to $-11.16^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. If $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}$ for water is $1.86 \mathrm{~K} /$ molal then calculate the amount of ice (in g ) that separates out on cooling the above solution.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B}}$ mass of ethylene glycol $(\mathrm{M}=62)=46.5 \mathrm{gm}$
$\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{A}}$ mass of water $=160 \mathrm{gm}$
$\Delta T_f$ depression in freezing point $=11.16 \mathrm{~K}$
Let $\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B}}$ be the mass of water remaining
$\Delta T_{\mathrm{f}}=\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{t}} \frac{\mathrm{~W}_{\mathrm{B}} \times 1000}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{B}} \mathrm{~W}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\prime}}$
$\begin{aligned}
& 11.16=\frac{1.86 \times 46.5 \times 1000}{62 \times \mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\prime}} \\
& \therefore \mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\prime}=125 \mathrm{gm}
\end{aligned}$

Mass of ice that separates out on cooling
$=W_B-W_B=160-125=35 \mathrm{gm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
1.8 g of ionic solid (that has CsCl type structure and completely dissociated in water) is present in 1 L aqueous solution and osmotic pressure of this solution is 4.92 atm at 300 K . The density of ionic solid is $30 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^3$ with edge length $x \mathrm{~nm}$. The value of 100 x is
$\left(R=0.082 \frac{\mathrm{Latm}}{\mathrm{Mol} \mathrm{~K}}, \mathrm{~N}_{\mathrm{A}}=6 \times 10^{23}\right)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \pi=\mathrm{i} \times \mathrm{C} \times \mathrm{R} \times \mathrm{T} \\ & \mathrm{T}=300 \mathrm{~K}, \mathrm{R}=0.082 \\ & \mathrm{i}=2 \\ & \mathrm{C}=\frac{4.92}{0.082 \times 300 \times 2}=0.1 \\ & 0.1=\frac{\text { Mol of salt }}{\text { Volume }} \\ & \text { Molar mass of salt }=18 \mathrm{~g} \\ & \mathrm{~d}=\frac{1 \times 18}{6 \times 10^{23} \times \mathrm{a}^3} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}^3=\frac{18}{6 \times 10^{23} \times 30}=10^{-24} \mathrm{~cm}^3 \\ & \mathrm{a}=10^{-8} \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \mathrm{a}=0.1 \mathrm{~nm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A solution of mono basic acid $\left(3 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{M}\right)$ has a freezing point of depression of $0.06^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Calculate pKa for the acid. $(\log 1.6992=0.2302)$ [Molal depression constant $1.86^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}$ for water]
questions.solution:
We know that, $\Delta T_f=\mathrm{i} \times \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{f}} \times \mathrm{m}$ Where $\mathrm{i}=$ Vant Hoff factor $\begin{aligned} & \Delta \mathrm{T}=0.06 \\ & \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{f}}=1.86 \\ & \mathrm{~m}=\text { molality } \end{aligned}$ Molality may be taken as molarity because solution is dilute and solvent is water. $=3 \times 10^{-2}$ Substituting the values in equation (i), we get, $0.06=\mathrm{i} \times 1.86 \times 3 \times 10^{-2}$ $\mathrm{i}=\frac{0.06}{1.86 \times 3 \times 10^{-2}}$ $i=1.07526$ Let acid is NH
$K_a=\frac{C \alpha \cdot C \alpha}{C(1-\alpha)}=C \alpha^2$ $\mathrm{i}=\frac{\text { Total mole after dissociation }}{\text { initial mole }}=\frac{\mathrm{C}(1-\alpha)+\mathrm{C} \alpha+\mathrm{C} \alpha}{\mathrm{C}}=1+\alpha$ $\alpha=\mathrm{i}-1$ $\alpha=1.07526-1$ $\alpha=0.07526$ $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=\mathrm{C} \alpha^2$ $=3 \times 10^{-2} \times(0.07526)^2$ $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}=1.6992 \times 10^{-4}$ $\mathrm{PK}_{\mathrm{a}}=-\log \mathrm{Ka}$ $=-\log \left(1.6992 \times 10^{-4}\right)$ $=+3.769$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
